items.q: Why did the Industrial Revolution begin in Britain?
items.correct: A combination of capital from trade and empire, coal and iron deposits, agricultural improvements, political stability, and a culture of invention.
items.misconception: Because James Watt invented the steam engine.
items.random_wrong: Because Britain banned slavery before other countries.
items.why: Inventions are effects of the conditions, not causes. Steam engines existed before Watt; what mattered was the economic and social environment that made them profitable to deploy at scale.

items.correct: A combination of moral campaigning, slave resistance and rebellions (especially Haiti and Jamaica), and the shifting economic interest of industrial capitalism.
items.misconception: Wilberforce single-handedly persuaded Parliament to end slavery out of pure morality.
items.random_wrong: Slavery was abolished because cotton was no longer profitable.
items.why: Students inherit the heroic Wilberforce narrative. Recent scholarship emphasises slave revolts (Haiti, Demerara, Jamaica) and economic shifts — abolition was not a gift from above.

items.q: Why did Britain win the Battle of Britain?
items.correct: Radar, the Dowding command system, home advantage in pilot recovery, German tactical errors (switching to bombing cities), and adequate fighter production.
items.misconception: The RAF won because Spitfires were better than any German aircraft.
items.random_wrong: Hitler called off the attack to invade the USSR immediately.
items.why: The Bf 109 was comparable to the Spitfire. Victory came from integrated air defence and German strategic mistakes, not aircraft superiority — a key point in modern historiography.
It's Robert Catesby. “Guy Fawkes” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Hampden. “Five Knights” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was Charles I executed?
items.why: The real figure is 1649 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
